display_name: ADMISSION-GATED HI-VOL (2026-07-14, AxiS: 'we need volume'). The VOLUME arm of the admission A/B. Same broad base as admission_gated_v1 (cloned from baseline_v1, unconditional/filters:null, which fired 854x — proof SOL volume is reachable; the issue was unconditional=never-green firehose=-$1456), but the gate is LIQUIDITY-ONLY (liquidity_usd>=34k) instead of liq+demand. Mine: liq>=34k keeps ~52% of the firehose (vs ~20% for the tight liq+buy_pressure gate) and still cuts never-green 59->49% / lifts win 21->25%. So this fires ~2.5x more than admission_gated_v1 at a modest quality trim. THE A/B question: does more volume net more fidelity-honest $, or does the extra never-green exposure eat it? Judge admission_gated_hivol (volume) vs admission_gated_v1 (quality) vs the fleet baseline on realized net at n>=30. PAPER, base $20. exits inherit the fleet ladder + enforced sol_bail. RH fires more only because its racers use broad entry modes; SOL's dip-waiters gate on no_dip+no_demand_turn -> this bot skips that (baseline breadth) to get RH-like throughput WITH a rug/never-green floor.
